reviews.review_description: Whenever I play the bats phase through the thing I’m hitting it makes a circle which means I hit it but the game doesn’t progress
The game is great to start but gets quite boring and I feel I didn’t get everything I wanted from the game
This game is very bed

This game does insert the neo-swastika rainbow flag as a background banner in some levels. The simplicity of this game seems geared at younger kids although I’m not saying adults can’t find it fun. Just be warned if you are trying to protect your kids from far-left pervasive symbolism based propaganda. It’s a cool game but obviously made by ideological activist who wanted to insert their symbolism.

Finished my home run today and it was absolutely out of this world how this game knocked it out of the park!! I was already a massive fan of the developers having played What the Golf, so when I saw this it was an instant buy even at full price and was the first game I've played on the Quest 3, and I haven't touched any of the other games yet because I've been glued to this one. Having played their other game there  were a few surprises that I saw coming, but far more where I still got the classic "what the..." moments.

I love these devopers so much! This game aims to be what all games should be, fun! And it hits it out of the park. There are so many different challenges that are never the same twice. I love the elephant that always spies on you! It's very charming, I will buy anything from this team. After I beat the game I started playing again from the beginning! Thank you!!

What the bat is a fun experience with good humor and some challenging levels. It’s a great way to spend 45mins to an hour, but you’ll only play it once. If you’re ok with that it’s a fun time, but if you want more content I’d recommend choosing a different game. I’d rate it an 8.7/10!

I don't know what the deal with that elephant thing is. I never understood what it's supposed to be, a quirky background character? If so they weren't even remotely funny in any of the chapters. If the elephant is supposed to be our sibling, roommate or whatever then it was poorly explained in the story.
The game's asking price is too steep for the game's longevity and it's way too casual. And what's the deal with LGBT flags being plastered all over the place? They're distracting and really don't fit the vision of the game, almost like the devs were forced to put them there.

developer_response.body: We are sorry that you didn't like it. I hope you find something just for you ❤️

reviews.review_title: A good game - just missing something
reviews.review_description:
I think this game is fantastic, but it lacks something that what the golf has.

The music is phenomenal. The sound is perfect for the environment and I love how when you look at the big blue square that calls itself an elephant it adds tuba/baritone/low brass (I'm not the best at identifying them) to the music, making it even better.

The gameplay is just fine until you get near the end, probably starting around 4-5 worlds from the end. After that, it gets back some of the charm that what the golf brings and is a whole lot better. All of the levels are fun, there aren't very many little secrets, references, and plays on words like there are in what the golf. I did notice a couple, such as the seagulls taking the pictures, but a very disappointing amount. I also really don't like the black screen in between each challenge/level, and I think it would be great to add the dad jokes and puns like what the golf has in between levels.

I think it is a fun concept of having bats for hands and the story for the most part works well and is enjoyable. The only real problem with the story itself is that it is a little short, but I knew that buying it so I can hardly complain.

Of course, the developers may have not wanted to make an exact copy of what the golf just on VR, and I respect that. I also know that this is a game that just got released, and there can be a whole lot more improvement to the game.

In short, this game is great and I am glad I bought it, just the littler details, and the finer aspects, are a little lacking.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review and all your thoughtful input. We will consider all this when planning content updates. We are super happy you like our game(s),. Feel free to join our Discord (then we can chat some more).  -Peter

reviews.review_title: I adore this quirky silly game so much😍
reviews.review_description: All the charm of What the Golf with a whole bunch off goofball room scale shenanigans- what isn’t there to love!

How awesome can it get to spend time *inside* the cute world of whacky mini-games and tongue-in-cheek jokes I knew from "What the Golf?", I wondered. Answer: Tremendously!

Thrilled for upcoming chapters!

reviews.review_title: You like What the Golf?, you like this!
reviews.review_description: This is a great little comedic romp through VR! A whole bunch of fun minigames that are fun the play through and require a variety of troubleshooting or skill. If you are looking for a narrative, this isn't your game. Just a fun, relaxing VR experience.
For anyone experiencing VR for the first time this game feels amazing! The sound, art style, and colors pop!
